primary.$description: Primary brand colour. Use for prominent brand expressions and key brand-coloured interactive elements.

support.$description: Supporting brand colour. A decorative flourish for accent strokes, brand patterns, and tints. Not contrast-safe; don't use for text, icons, or interactive elements.

default.$description: Logo colour in the current surface. Use as the fill for the brand mark.

neutral.$description: Neutral logo colour. Use when the branded logo colour would clash with surrounding content (e.g. over imagery, in monochrome contexts).

default.$description: Default page background. Use for the main canvas behind content.

panel.$description: Elevated surface background. Use for cards, modals, and other contained surfaces that sit above the page.

subtle.$description: Low-contrast surface background. Use for small discrete surfaces (inline code, zebra stripes, disabled inputs) that need light differentiation.

scrim.$description: Translucent overlay. Use as a backdrop behind overlay surfaces (e.g. modals, drawers) to dim underlying content while keeping it partially visible.

default.$description: Default foreground colour. Use for primary UI text and icons.

prose.$description: Body copy colour. Use for paragraphs, descriptions, and any reading content. Slightly softer than the default foreground for visual comfort.

text.$description: Muted text colour. Use for secondary text (helper copy, metadata, captions, disabled labels) where lower emphasis is appropriate.

nonText.$description: Muted foreground for non-text decorations (icons, dividers, indicator dots). Reduced contrast. Non-text shapes don't require text-grade contrast minimums.

contrast.$description: Contrast foreground. Use for text and icons placed over saturated coloured fills (brand, interactive, status, avatar) where the default foreground would lack sufficient contrast.

default.$description: Default border colour. Use for standard component borders and dividers (cards, inputs, tables).

brand.$description: Brand-tinted border. Use to associate a bordered element with the brand (interactive cards, code blocks, branded section dividers).

subtle.$description: Low-contrast border. Use for divider lines and separators that should recede visually.

default.$description: Default interactive colour. Use for links and primary actionable elements in their resting state.

visited.$description: Visited interactive colour for links the user has already followed. Inherits the default interactive colour.

hover.$description: Hover state colour for interactive elements. Use on pointer hover to signal affordance.

focus.$description: Focus indicator colour. Use for focus rings and keyboard-focus outlines on any interactive element.

active.$description: Active (pressed) state colour. Use during click or tap to signal activation.

default.$description: Neutral interactive colour. Use for actionable elements that shouldn't compete with brand-coloured interactive elements (neutral buttons, secondary controls, menu items).

visited.$description: Visited state colour for neutral interactive elements. Inherits the default neutral interactive colour.

hover.$description: Hover state colour for neutral interactive elements.

focus.$description: Focus state for neutral interactive elements. Inherits the standard interactive focus colour for consistency across element types.

active.$description: Active (pressed) state colour for neutral interactive elements.

default.$description: Inline link colour for prose. Pairs with `foreground.prose`. Tuned for sufficient contrast against the softer prose text colour.

hover.$description: Hover state colour for prose links.

hoverDecoration.$description: Underline colour for prose links on hover.

focus.$description: Focus state colour for prose links.

active.$description: Active (pressed) state colour for prose links.

activeDecoration.$description: Underline colour for prose links during active (pressed) state.

default.$description: Error status colour. Use for error icons, badges, alert borders, and any element conveying failure or destructive action.

subtle.$description: Subtle error background. Use as the fill behind error messages and alerts.

default.$description: Informational status colour. Use for info icons, badges, and alerts conveying neutral notifications.

subtle.$description: Subtle info background. Use as the fill behind info messages and alerts.

default.$description: Success status colour. Use for success icons, confirmation indicators, and positive outcome messaging.

subtle.$description: Subtle success background. Use as the fill behind success messages and alerts.

default.$description: Warning status colour. Use for caution icons, badges, and alerts requiring user attention but not signalling failure.

subtle.$description: Subtle warning background. Use as the fill behind warning messages and alerts.

00.$description: Base16 palette slot 00. Default background for code blocks and syntax-highlighted regions.

01.$description: Base16 palette slot 01. Lighter background variant (panels, gutters in code views).

03.$description: Base16 palette slot 03. Comments, invisibles, and line highlights.

04.$description: Base16 palette slot 04. Dark foreground for status bars and chrome around code blocks.

05.$description: Base16 palette slot 05. Default foreground colour for code text.

06.$description: Base16 palette slot 06. Light foreground variant for emphasised tokens.

07.$description: Base16 palette slot 07. Light background variant.

08.$description: Base16 palette slot 08. Variables, errors, and deletions.

09.$description: Base16 palette slot 09. Integers, booleans, constants, and numeric literals.

0a.$description: Base16 palette slot 0a. Classes, search highlight backgrounds.

0b.$description: Base16 palette slot 0b. Strings, additions, and inherited colours.

0c.$description: Base16 palette slot 0c. Regular expressions and escape characters.

0d.$description: Base16 palette slot 0d. Functions, methods, and headings.

0e.$description: Base16 palette slot 0e. Keywords, storage, and language directives.

0f.$description: Base16 palette slot 0f. Deprecated, opening/closing tags, and embedded language markers.

default.$description: Default avatar background. Use as a fallback when no palette slot has been assigned. Slots 01–09 provide a palette for distinguishable per-user backgrounds when no uploaded image is available.
